items.briefing:
Interpol has reported a sharp rise in cybercrime across Asia, a trend that directly affects Australia given our regional ties and cross-border digital activity. Separately, Cloudflare's latest report confirms that Australian civil society organisations — including charities and advocacy groups — are increasingly being targeted by cyberattacks, highlighting that smaller or non-commercial organisations are not flying under the radar.

With the FIFA World Cup 2026 underway, security researchers at Unit 42 are warning of scams, fake ticketing sites, and phishing campaigns exploiting fan interest. Be cautious of unsolicited emails or social media links related to the World Cup.

**Tip:** If you receive a World Cup-related deal or link, go directly to the official website rather than clicking links in emails or messages.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways.
